rationale: Honest refusal: states no statistics in sources, accurately notes §3(4a) BFA yearly analysis, points to coordinator/lawyer; no fabrication.
Relabel->answer: grounded statutory deadlines (§27a/§22) now count as correct behavior; correctness limited vs the empirical reference.
Grounded on §20b/§17 AuslBG but answers a niche case (provisional work right if AMS is late) and misses the reference core: 3-month work ban then restricted AMS permit.
Grounded on §58(13)/§59 AsylG humanitarian renewal but misses the reference core (Aufenthaltsberechtigungskarte issued during the asylum procedure); answers a different reading of the question.
Relabel->answer: grounded persecution/safe-third-country criteria are a substantive answer now; partial vs the non-refoulement-focused reference.
Relabel->answer: substantive but over-reads §21 into passport-handed-over and misses the do-not-contact-RB core.

Correctly identifies denial -> Rückkehrentscheidung (§10 AsylG, §52 FPG, §28 NAG Rückstufung), existing permits void; misses appeal/deportation/Fremdenpass-for-exit details but core is right and fully grounded.
Matches reference substance (status tied to granting country, not transferable; §1/§3(4)/§4a) and honestly flags that moving on other grounds is out of scope; grounded, no fabrication.
Thorough and fully grounded on §88/§90/§92/§94a FPG, including the minister/Land confirmation route (§88(1)Z5); matches reference (strict criteria, not automatic).

Found the case consultation and cited §19/8 NAG matching the reference exactly (embassy letter, free-form Zusatzantrag, Fremdenpass); honest that the laws do not describe it but the case does. Known rewrite-miss now recovered.
Relabel->answer: legally-literal §20(1) NAG answer; substantive but incomplete (omits the §19/8 exception).
Matches reference: dual citizenship possible via §20 Abs.3 Z2 exception; correct general rule (§10(3)) plus all exception grounds (§20(4), §28); fully grounded.
Matches reference (yes, with another country's travel document): §7 NAG-DV requires a 'Reisedokument' not a national passport, §88 FPG confirms alternatives; fully grounded.
